title: Studio Standards
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Defines the permanent studio-wide standards governing focused, repeatable, verified work across the reconciled TMS-OS repository and documentation system.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document defines the permanent studio-wide standards for Two Marshalls Studios. It recovers the working standards established from the beginning of the project and places them in one governed human-readable record.
These standards apply across studio operations, development, documentation, recovery, testing, source control, and future work unless a later verified decision formally changes them.

sections.title: Permanent Standards Rule
paragraphs: Studio standards must be followed consistently and must not depend on one chat thread, one workstation, one person's memory, or temporary instructions.
When a standard changes, the change must be approved, recorded, and reconciled with the permanent records affected by it.

sections.title: Standard 001 — Stay Focused on the Approved Studio Vision
items: Work only on the agreed current goal and approved studio vision.
Do not introduce unrelated systems, features, redesigns, or improvements during active build work.
If a useful enhancement appears, preserve it in ENH-LOG-001 rather than interrupting approved work.
A separate enhancement path begins only when Stephen explicitly approves it.

sections.title: Standard 002 — Do Not Skip Steps
items: Instructions must proceed in a controlled sequence.
State exactly where to go before asking for an action.
State exactly what to click, enter, move, rename, test, or verify.
Do not assume a step has been completed without evidence.
Do not jump ahead to later work before the current checkpoint passes.

sections.title: Standard 003 — Use One Controlled Step at a Time
items: During active build and recovery work, give the next controlled action rather than a large uncontrolled batch.
Wait for the result or screenshot when verification is required.
Review the actual result before deciding the next step.
If the result is wrong, correct it before continuing.

sections.title: Standard 004 — Use Complete Files for Code Changes
items: When a code file must change, provide the complete updated file.
Do not provide partial snippets or fragments as the normal change method.
State the exact file path and file name.
Explain where the complete file belongs.
Test the result after replacement and state the expected result.

sections.title: Standard 005 — Stop and Fix the Source of Bugs
paragraphs: PROC-002 - Bug Fix Procedure governs the full controlled bug-fix process.
items: When a bug appears, stop unrelated new work.
Record the expected result and the actual result.
Reproduce the problem when possible.
Identify the source of the problem.
Make the smallest approved correction that fixes the source.
Retest the original failure and related behavior before continuing.

sections.title: Standard 006 — Test Before Declaring Work Complete
items: A change is not complete merely because it was entered or uploaded.
Run the required test.
Compare the actual result with the expected result.
Use screenshots, system output, record counts, or other evidence when appropriate.
Do not mark a checkpoint as passed until the result is verified.

sections.title: Standard 007 — Preserve Work Across Multiple Workstations
items: Important studio work must not be limited to one computer.
Source code and project history must be preserved through the approved shared source-control process.
Permanent human-readable documentation must be preserved in the approved Google Headquarters structure.
The studio must maintain a clear process for continuing work from another location or workstation.

sections.title: Standard 008 — Maintain Clear Source-of-Truth Boundaries
items: TMS-OS and StudioDB govern structured studio records.
The Studio Portal presents and exposes governed information.
GitHub preserves source code and project history.
Google Headquarters preserves permanent human-readable documentation.
Chat history, screenshots, temporary uploads, local files, and backups may provide evidence but must not become competing permanent sources of truth.

sections.title: Standard 009 — Preserve the Verified Modular TMS-OS Architecture
items: ARCH-001 is the authoritative description of the current verified architecture.

The architecture includes the Studio Portal, modular StudioDB records, shared rendering, shared search, structured permanent documents, work-session governance, document writers, transaction analysis, rollback support, lifecycle controls, and state-transition controls.
Data responsibilities must remain separated according to the approved modular structure.
Shared behavior must be used where the architecture already provides shared rendering, search, document access, session control, or governed output handling.
Do not create isolated competing systems, duplicate sources of truth, or uncontrolled permanent-write paths without explicit approval.

sections.title: Standard 010 — Protect Verified Stable Checkpoints
items: Preserve the last known stable state before risky or structural changes.
Do not overwrite verified working behavior without a controlled reason.
After a change, verify that previously working behavior still works.
Record important completed milestones and stable checkpoints in the appropriate permanent records.

sections.title: Standard 011 — Document Important Completed Work
items: A work session is not fully closed until important results are captured where they belong.
Preserve significant changes, tests, decisions, bugs, fixes, lessons, milestones, and next actions.
Use PROC-003 - Documentation Update Procedure for controlled permanent documentation updates.
Do not allow important knowledge to remain only in chat.

sections.title: Standard 012 — Verify Permanent Documentation in Every Approved Source
items: The structured JSON document in pages/documents is the repository implementation record for each portal document.
The Studio Portal viewer must render the permanent document correctly before the repository checkpoint is accepted.
The document catalog, search behavior, title, version, last-updated value, and related metadata must be verified when applicable.
Google Headquarters may continue to preserve permanent human-readable documentation, but its publication status must be tracked separately from repository verification.
Temporary uploads, local copies, screenshots, chat records, and backups must not be counted as verified permanent records.
Update DOC-INDEX-001, DOC-STATE-001, and STATE-001 only after the applicable verification evidence exists.

sections.title: Standard 013 — Preserve Historical Accuracy
items: Recover history from verified evidence.
Do not invent dates, milestones, decisions, or completed work.
Separate verified history from future ideas.
Keep unresolved or unverified historical material identified as such until evidence supports it.
Preserve earlier records when later decisions supersede them rather than silently deleting history.

sections.title: Standard 014 — Capture Enhancements Without Derailing Current Work
items: Capture enhancement ideas when they appear.
Maintain the enhancement history from the beginning of the project.
Record ideas in ENH-LOG-001 with enough context for later review.
Do not build an enhancement during unrelated approved work unless Stephen explicitly approves the change in direction.

sections.title: Standard 015 — Use Clear Names, IDs, and Locations
items: Permanent records must use controlled document IDs.
File and document names must match the approved name exactly.
Permanent documents must be stored in the approved Google Headquarters location.
Temporary files must not be confused with verified permanent records.
Duplicate IDs and competing permanent copies are not acceptable.

sections.title: Standard 016 — Use Numeric Specifications for Asset Work
items: For studio asset creation, use exact numeric dimensions and values whenever the software supports them.
State exactly where each number is entered.
Include dimensions, transforms, bevel values, and other measurable settings when relevant.
Use this method to make asset creation repeatable and easier to verify.

sections.title: Standard 017 — Maintain the Enhancement Ideas Log
items: ENH-LOG-001 is a permanent studio record.
Enhancements must be collected from the beginning of the project, not only from the date the log was created.
New enhancement ideas must continue to be captured as they arise.
The log must remain separate from active approved build scope.

sections.title: Standard 018 — Use Controlled Decision Records
items: Major decisions must be preserved in DEC-LOG-001.
A major decision must record what was decided and its operating effect.
Future ideas must not be recorded as completed decisions.
When a decision affects architecture, roadmap, state, procedures, or other permanent records, those records must also be reconciled.

sections.title: Standard 019 — Complete Documentation Reconciliation Before Uncontrolled Forward Development
items: Documentation Reconciliation is the current approved milestone for Work Session 086.
Permanent documentation must be reconciled against verified repository evidence while preserving architecture, history, governance, and document responsibilities.
Feature development and unrelated enhancements remain paused until the approved reconciliation checkpoint is complete.
Do not abandon the controlled reconciliation sequence unless Stephen explicitly changes the approved priority.

sections.title: Current Standards Checkpoint
items: Current repository version: v0.28.1
Current work session: WS086 — Enterprise Documentation Reconciliation
Current milestone: Documentation Reconciliation
Current phase: Permanent Documentation Alignment
Verified structured permanent document inventory: 20 JSON documents
Current architecture authority: ARCH-001
Current state authority: STATE-001
Current roadmap authority: ROADMAP-001
Documentation health authority: DOC-STATE-001
Documentation inventory authority: DOC-INDEX-001
Feature development: Paused pending completion of the approved reconciliation checkpoint

sections.title: Standard Change Control
items: Identify the proposed standard change.
Determine whether it is a correction, replacement, or new standard.
Obtain explicit approval when the change affects studio direction or established operating practice.
Record the related decision in DEC-LOG-001 when applicable.
Update this document through PROC-003.
Reconcile all affected permanent records.
Verify the final permanent Google documentation.

sections.title: Relationship to Other Permanent Records
items: PROC-001 governs controlled work sessions.
PROC-002 governs bug fixing.
PROC-003 governs documentation updates.
PROC-004 governs its assigned controlled operating workflow.
DEC-LOG-001 preserves major decisions.
ENH-LOG-001 preserves deferred enhancement ideas.
ARCH-001 defines the current architecture.
ARCH-EVO-001 preserves architecture evolution.
STATE-001 records the current system checkpoint.
ROADMAP-001 records approved strategic direction and milestone sequencing.
DOC-STATE-001 records documentation health and reconciliation status.
DOC-INDEX-001 controls the permanent document inventory and document responsibilities.
WS-HIST-001 preserves work-session history.
MILE-HIST-001 preserves milestone history.
RISK-001 preserves active and historical risks.
TECH-DEBT-001 preserves technical debt.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: Two Marshalls Studios will use consistent, controlled, repeatable standards so work remains focused, verifiable, recoverable, documented, and accessible across sessions and workstations.
These standards preserve the operating discipline established from the beginning of the project and must continue to govern future studio work unless formally changed through the controlled process.
